scope: Extend the CLI todo application with status changes and deletion: done (mark task completed), delete (remove task), and clear (remove all tasks). Build on existing help, add, and list commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'done <id>' marks the specified task as completed
Running 'list' displays tasks with their completion status
Running 'delete <id>' removes the specified task from storage
Running 'clear' removes all tasks from storage with user confirmation
Data persists correctly after done, delete, and clear operations
Clear error messages shown for invalid task IDs
nonGoals: Undone command to mark tasks back to pending
Filtering by completion status in list command
Bulk operations (delete multiple tasks at once)
Priority levels, due dates, or tagging
qaChecks: Done command updates task status that appears in subsequent list output
Delete command removes task that no longer appears in list output
Clear command removes all tasks, resulting in empty list output
Clear command requires user confirmation before executing
Clear error shown when done/delete called with non-existent ID
Data file remains valid JSON after done, delete, or clear operations
Existing add and list commands continue to work unchanged
taskBreakdown: Implement task status model and done command to mark tasks completed
Implement delete command to remove individual tasks by ID and clear command to remove all tasks with confirmation
definitionOfDone: CLI executable supports done, delete, and clear commands in addition to existing help, add, and list. Done marks tasks as completed and list displays status. Delete removes specified tasks. Clear removes all tasks with confirmation. All operations persist correctly to local JSON storage with appropriate error handling.
